acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI compiles with surface IPC imports
- [ ] No direct `crate::daemon::ipc` in CLI
- [ ] Existing IPC behavior unchanged
description:
**Problem**
CLI commands import IPC contracts from `crate::daemon::ipc` instead of surface.
design: Replace IPC types/helpers with `beads_surface::ipc::*` across CLI modules with no behavior change.
title: Migrate CLI IPC imports to beads-surface

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI subscribe connects and prints events in order.
- [ ] CLI handles retryable timeout errors and exits cleanly.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes for CLI tests if present.
description:
**Problem**
Phase 7: add CLI support for streaming subscriptions. Without a CLI command, subscribe is hard to validate and use.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.3 (CLI changes)
**Files:** src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, src/cli/render.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a `bd subscribe` command with flags for namespace and require_min_seen.
- Stream events to stdout in json and human modes (consistent with other commands).
- Propagate retryable errors and exit with non zero status on permanent errors.
title: Phase 7: CLI subscribe command

description:
**Problem**
beads-rs wraps every JSON response as \`{\"result\":\"issue\",\"data\":{...}}\` (scalar) or \`{\"result\":\"issues\",\"data\":[...]}\` (list). Go emits bare \`{...}\` or \`[...]\`. Gascity's \`parseIssuesTolerant\` calls \`extractJSON\` expecting bare arrays/objects; it cannot walk into \`.data\`. Every command gascity calls fails JSON parsing as a result.

Confirmed via live capture on 2026-04-20 in /tmp/rs-id-test:

\`\`\`
$ bd show <id> --json
{\"result\":\"issue\",\"data\":{\"id\":\"...\",...}}
$ bd list --json
{\"result\":\"issues\",\"data\":[{...}]}
\`\`\`

Reference: docs/go-parity/cmds/gascity/00-envelope.md.

**Design**
Project the wrapped response to bare object/array at the CLI wire layer (not the IPC/daemon layer — internal consumers that want metadata can keep the wrapper).

Per-command Go convention (to preserve):

- \`bd create\` → bare object \`{...}\`
- \`bd show <id>\` → bare **array** \`[{...}]\` (Go wraps single-id results in an array too!)
- \`bd list\` → bare array \`[{...},{...}]\`
- \`bd update\`, \`bd close\`, \`bd delete\`, \`bd reopen\` → bare object or array (match Go)
- \`bd ready\` → see separate bead; has nested \`{issues, blocked_count, closed_count}\` shape that needs its own fix
- \`bd count\`, \`bd blocked\`, \`bd stale\`, \`bd deleted\` → match Go shapes
- \`bd dep add\`, \`bd dep remove\` → match Go (probably bare object or empty)

Error responses stay a bare \`{\"error\":\"...\"}\` object, matching Go.

Reference captures of Go v1.0.2 shapes are in cmds/gascity/*.md.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`bd show <id> --json | jq -e '.[0].id'\` works (array of objects)
- [ ] \`bd create \"t\" --json | jq -e '.id'\` works (bare object)
- [ ] \`bd list --json | jq -e 'length'\` works (bare array)
- [ ] Every command listed in cmds/gascity/*.md matches its documented Go shape (update captures if the current doc is stale)
- [ ] Mutation error path still returns \`{\"error\":\"...\"}\` with non-zero exit
- [ ] \`cargo xtest\` green; no regressions in the CLI render tests

**Independent of**
All other Floor 0 work. Each wire-field-level bead (issue_type, owner, RFC3339, deps flatten) is a separate change.

**Files likely touched**
- \`crates/beads-cli/src/render.rs\` (or wherever final JSON emission happens)
- Per-command result type conversions
- CLI render tests
title: Floor 0 wire: drop {result,data} envelope on scalar/list JSON output
